title: Core inclusion gamma_c ⊆ N_c is assumed, not proved (circular citation of the goal)

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body: Theorem `thm:hall-full` ('Hall basis theorem, full normal-closure form') states verbatim gamma_c(F)=<B_c>^F=N_c, which IS the theorem to be proved (`thm:main`). The proof of `thm:main` in §sec:final simply 'invokes Theorem thm:hall-full'. The alternative route via `prop:reduce` needs N_{c+1}⊆N_c for all c (eq:onestep-goal), which is itself only supplied by thm:hall-full. The standard NUMBERED references actually cited (M. Hall Thm 11.2.4; MKS Thm 5.13A) are the GRADED Hall theorem (= prop:hall), NOT the normal-closure form. So thm:hall-full is the unproved heart of the problem relabeled as an external citation. rem:noncircular's defense ('its proof is the collection process') does not reproduce that proof. Net: gamma_c ⊆ N_c is unproved.

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body: FILED IN ERROR / RESOLVED. This issue concerns problem rm-03102 (free groups, lower central series, Hall basic commutators: is gamma_c(F)=<B_c>^F?), NOT q7 (uniform lattices with 2-torsion). The grading task referenced problem id 'rm-03102', which the tracker does not recognize (only q1–q10 exist), and a fallback POST landed on q7 by mistake. Ignore here. Resolving to keep q7 clean.
